free_text: Studies of the mass distribution in fission of uranium
induced by 170 MeV protons.
High purity uranium foils were used.
A 50 cm**3 Ge(Li) Detector.
Gas flow proportional counter for beta-emission
from the Sn- and Mo-samples.
The complex decay curve of tin samples were
followed for four to five months and resolved into
three components, viz.  121-G-Sn,123-G-Sn and 125-G-Sn.
The component 123-G-Sn was determined after complete
decay of 125-G-Sn and chemical removal of 125-Sb.
The Gustaf Werner Institute in Uppsala,
the Synchrocyclotron.
Irradiation time was from 4 to 5 hours.
/by Authors/ Formation cross-sections are all measured
relative to Mo-99 with an absolute fission yield
determined to 56+-6 mb. See MONIT-REF.
The errors quoted are those based on
uncertainties in activity measurements, chemical yield
determination and counting efficiency.
For mass region A=105 to A=140.
Date is corrected
Subentries O0562008,010 are corrected
according to O.Schwerer's remarks.
Last checking has been done.
SD: REACTION code corrected in Subent 2.
The yield given for 113-Sn was corrected for the
branched decay of 113-m-Sn.
- By Author - The yield given for 113-Sn can be
regarded as independent yield.  This since the
contribution from 113-Sb must be negligibly small
whatever charge distribution and dispersion govern this
mass region.
SD: Illegal isom. frag combination -M+G
deleted from SF4 of REACTION code.
- By Author - The Yield of 117-M-Sn is Independent,
Since Neither 117-In Nor 117-Sb Populate the 117-Sn
Isomer State.
The Authors Assumed Sigma(117-M-SN)/Sigma(117-G-SN)>2
To Calculate Total Independent Yield of 117-Sn.
Gamma Abundance Determined in this Work 0.0060+-0.0005.
The Data
Are
Used to Calculate Ratio of Cumulative Cross Sections
For Sn-123/Sn-125.
Abundances Determined in this Work 0.0945+-0.0080.
Lower Limit of Chain Yield. The Value is Based
On Measured Yield, Without Taking Into Account
Contribution from Isomer.
The Value is Based on 125-Sb Only.
Uncertainty of Misc1. Noany Analysis is
Presented.
Uncerainty of Misc2.  Noany Analysis is
Presented.
